shell.localeHint: Language and theme selections now sync from the account, cookies, and system defaults.

auth.title: Sign in to ExamOps
auth.subtitle: This milestone authenticates against seeded database users and persists active sessions in PostgreSQL.
auth.error: The email or password was incorrect.
auth.credentialsBody: Run the Prisma seed, then sign in with one of the app users below. The shared seed password comes from SEED_APP_USERS_PASSWORD.
auth.arabicPanelBody: Arabic and English authentication flows now share the same database-backed session layer.

dashboard.title: Operations overview
dashboard.subtitle: The shell now resolves locale, theme, and authentication state from live database-backed preferences.

shell.title: Shared shell
shell.body: Dashboard routes share one protected layout, responsive navigation, and a motion-aware header.

auth.title: Persisted sessions
auth.body: Login and logout create and revoke session records in PostgreSQL instead of relying on environment-only credentials.

theme.title: Adaptive appearance
theme.body: Light, dark, and system themes now resolve from account preferences, settings, and cookies.

copy.title: Locale-aware copy
copy.body: Shell copy is centralized so Arabic and English can expand without scattering strings through the tree.

team.title: Team access model
team.subtitle: This screen reflects the current role guard strategy and where shell permissions already apply.

matrix.super_admin: Full platform access, including settings and future user administration.
matrix.coordinator: Operational access with management visibility across shell sections.
matrix.senior: Protected shell access ready for attendance and evaluation slices.

settings.title: Foundation settings preview
settings.subtitle: This route is intentionally restricted to super admins and coordinators to prove the role guard path.

items.auth: Auth reads active app users, password hashes, and persisted sessions from PostgreSQL.
items.locale: Language and theme preferences can be stored in the database and mirrored to cookies for rendering.
items.next: Locations CRUD is the next expansion point after the remaining shell foundation polish.

locations.title: Locations hierarchy
locations.subtitle: Browse the active location structure across governorates, universities, buildings, floors, and rooms.
locations.description: This tree is powered by the real locations API and respects soft-deactivation by default.
locations.searchPlaceholder: Search by Arabic or English name
locations.searchEmptyBody: Try a different Arabic or English search term to filter the hierarchy.
locations.emptyBody: Create location records through the API or seed data to populate the hierarchy.
locations.errorBody: The locations API did not return data. Check authentication and database connectivity, then try again.
locations.statsBody: Counts update from the live API response each time the tree reloads.

importFlow.title: Import locations
importFlow.subtitle: Upload a CSV file to create or reuse locations row by row without stopping on the first invalid row.
importFlow.sampleBody: Every row represents one hierarchy path. Partial rows are allowed if the chain stops cleanly at the deepest provided level.
importFlow.missingFile: Choose a CSV file before starting the import.
importFlow.unsupportedFile: Only CSV files are supported for locations import.

proctors.title: Proctors directory
proctors.subtitle: Browse live proctor records backed by the ExamOps users table.
proctors.description: This screen reads from the protected proctors API, supports bilingual search, and keeps import, export, and profile review in one responsive workspace.
proctors.searchPlaceholder: Search by Arabic or English name, phone, email, or national ID
proctors.snapshotBody: Summary cards update from the live list response so import and export work against the same source of truth.
proctors.listBody: Showing {count} proctors from the live API response.
proctors.detailBody: Select a proctor to inspect profile details, status, and operational counts.
proctors.emptyBody: Create proctor records through the API or import tools to populate this directory.
proctors.errorBody: The proctors API did not return list data. Check authentication and database connectivity, then try again.
proctors.detailErrorTitle: Could not load the selected proctor
proctors.detailErrorBody: The selected proctor could not be loaded from the API.
proctors.detailEmptyBody: Choose a proctor from the list to view the full profile.
proctors.noNotes: No notes are stored for this proctor.

importFlow.title: Import proctors
importFlow.subtitle: Upload a CSV file to create new proctors or safely reuse existing records row by row.
importFlow.sampleBody: The template supports bilingual names, phone normalization, optional governorate matching, and partial success reporting.
importFlow.missingFile: Choose a CSV file before starting the import.
importFlow.unsupportedFile: Only CSV files are supported for proctors import.

exportFlow.title: Export proctors
exportFlow.subtitle: Download the current directory in localized CSV or Excel format with optional status and governorate filters.

profile.subtitle: Operational history and performance summary for the selected proctor.
profile.summaryBody: These totals are aggregated in one service call across assignments, attendance, evaluations, and blocks.
profile.overviewBody: Core identity and status fields stay localized with the same fallback rules used across the directory.
profile.assignmentsBody: Session placement, role distribution, and manual override history.
profile.attendanceBody: Recorded attendance events for the proctor's assignments.
profile.evaluationsBody: Performance evaluations submitted against this proctor.
profile.blocksBody: All current and historical block records for this proctor.
profile.emptyAssignments: No assignment history is available for this proctor.
profile.emptyAttendance: No attendance history is available for this proctor.
profile.emptyEvaluations: No evaluations are available for this proctor.
profile.emptyBlocks: No block history is available for this proctor.
